comment: This is generated by ESQL's TBucketTests. Do not edit it. See docs/reference/query-languages/esql/README.md for how to regenerate it.
name: tbucket
description:
Creates groups of values - buckets - out of a `@timestamp` attribute.
The size of the buckets can be provided directly as a duration or period.
Alternatively, the bucket size can be chosen based on a recommended count
and a range.

When using ES|QL in Kibana, the range can be derived automatically from the
`@timestamp` filter
that Kibana adds to the query.

params.name: buckets
params.description: Target number of buckets, or desired bucket size. When a number is provided, the actual bucket size is derived from `from`/`to` or the `@timestamp` range in the query filter. When a duration or period is provided, it is used as the explicit bucket size.

params.name: buckets
params.description: Target number of buckets, or desired bucket size. When a number is provided, the actual bucket size is derived from `from`/`to` or the `@timestamp` range in the query filter. When a duration or period is provided, it is used as the explicit bucket size.

params.description: Target number of buckets, or desired bucket size. When a number is provided, the actual bucket size is derived from `from`/`to` or the `@timestamp` range in the query filter. When a duration or period is provided, it is used as the explicit bucket size.

params.name: from
params.description: Start of the range. Required with a numeric `buckets` when no `@timestamp` range is in the query filter.

params.name: to
params.description: End of the range. Required with a numeric `buckets` when no `@timestamp` range is in the query filter.

examples:
FROM sample_data
| STATS count = COUNT(*) BY bucket = TBUCKET(3, "2023-10-23T12:00:00Z", "2023-10-23T14:00:00Z")
| SORT bucket

FROM sample_data
| STATS count = COUNT(*) BY bucket = TBUCKET(20, "2023-10-23T12:00:00Z", "2023-10-23T14:00:00Z")
| SORT bucket

FROM sample_data
| STATS min = MIN(@timestamp), max = MAX(@timestamp) BY bucket = TBUCKET(1 hour)
| SORT min

FROM sample_data
| STATS min = MIN(@timestamp), max = MAX(@timestamp) BY bucket = TBUCKET("1 hour")
| SORT min
